category.name: zoo.window.shopRoadsInfo
category.item: Gravel road
This gravel path connects your zoo enclosures. A quality network of paths helps your visitors find their way and leads them to all the enclosures.
Blue and white sidewalk
This blue and white cobblestone path not only looks great, but it's also a great surface for strollers, scooters and roller skates. Smooth movement and quality fun, that's great!
Sand road
There are rarely paved roads in the steppe. You want your zoo to resemble the wild!
Pink and white sidewalk
How lovely: This stone path made of precious pink mosaic stones is something truly special. The perfect catwalk for visitors in high heels.
Black and white sidewalk
Black and white precious stones in the form of a mosaic beautifully line this path. They are worked with great care and become a jewel of your zoo.
The rocky road
Just like in the wild, small tufts of grass grow from the gaps between the stones. Not for the pedantic, but nature lovers will certainly enjoy it.
Pebble path
This gravel path looks nice and I'm sure no one will slip on it. Safe and pretty, what more could you want?
Dirt road
Dirt paths are popular in Africa. They add authenticity to your zoo and will make many animals feel at home.
Waterway
Splash, splash, splash! Put on your rubber boots, inflate your rubber boat and grab a rubber duck - there will be a lot of water on this trip!
Ice road
Yay! An ice road - that's going to be fun! Your visitors will have fun sliding down the frozen road. Be careful, it's not salted!
Savannah Road
A well-trodden path winds through the savannah, making it ideal for searching for tracks as on a safari.
Desert road
Under the scorching heat of the sun, sand blows across the desert, obliterating any traces.
Stone path
This path is quite bumpy. There are large stones and boulders on it, so good footwear is a must. Be careful not to fall!
Tundra Trail
Your visitors will need a compass on this trail, but it is also wise to stick to the beaten path.
Snow road
Sticky snow lies on the snow path, which your visitors will trudge along in their warm boots. Everything is so beautiful here!
Desert trail
In the wilderness, it's best to stay on the road. There are toxins and waste everywhere. Rubber boots with good soles are a must!
Alpine route
This hiking trail will take you up and down through the Alps. Good shoes with solid ankle support will protect you from slipping!
Cave path
Keep your eyes open so you don't slip. Maybe you'll spot a glittering grain of gold among the stones.
Forest path
On rainy days this path can be a bit muddy, but in the summer the forest is absolutely idyllic.
Coastal road
Walking barefoot on sparkling sand, shells, seaweed and occasionally some starfish is very beneficial for your health!
Magical trail
The bright blue trail, which is out of this world, may seem scary, but it is also adventurous. Where does it lead?
Sugar Land Trail
Crumb! Oops! Did you step on a candy? Be careful, there are chewing gums on the way! How beautifully colorful it is here!
Bamboo forest trail
The fog drifts through a vast, almost impenetrable bamboo forest. The tapping of a woodpecker can be heard in the distance. Simply idyllic!
Forest trail
If you don't want to swing from vine to vine in this jungle, you can walk along this well-trodden path. Be careful: From time to time, a snake or spider may cross the path.
The path through the swamp
Your visitors should remember to stay on the right path in the swamp. One wrong step and they'll fall into the mud!
Wooden platform
There's nothing better than walking along a wooden pier and hearing the creaking of a ship's deck. Your visitors will have a true coastal experience.
